verdicts.reason: The implementation correctly parses the specified markdown table format for build constraints, handling expected edge cases like missing sections, malformed rows, and incorrect ID sets.

The script correctly validates the structure of the Build Constraints section in program.md without introducing new attack surfaces; file path injection concern was explicitly overridden by human operator.
The new section in program.md is clearly introduced and well-structured as a markdown table, and the validation script provides clear, actionable feedback.

The new 'Build Constraints' section in program.md clearly documents the purpose, usage, and parsing format of the check_constraints.py script, making it highly discoverable for both human users and AI agents.
This project provides a critical self-validation mechanism for the system's core rules, ensuring consistency and machine-readability of build constraints.
verdicts.evidence: This tool will be integrated into every build process, acting as a foundational check for the integrity of the system's own governance document (program.md). It solves the problem of ensuring that the 'machine-checkable build constraints' are themselves structurally correct and parseable by machines, preventing errors in rule definition.

verdicts.reason: The tool's signature move is its highly specific, 'unreasonably good' validation of project-specific build constraints defined in a human-readable markdown table, effectively making documentation machine-checkable code.
No documented lessons or anti-patterns were violated by the addition of the build constraints section in program.md or the check_constraints.py script.
